summary: Foundational tick layer all other systems compose on. Defines the canonical Entity shape (a single flat struct used for ships/asteroids/stations/projectiles/pickups/drones/wrecks), a fixed-timestep 60 Hz simulation with an accumulator and render interpolation, semi-Newtonian flight physics (thrust accel + linear drag giving a soft max speed, instant-ish rotation toward mouse aim, boost/afterburner, strafe), keyboard-move + mouse-aim control, a spatial-hash broad phase with circle/circle response, and a tilted top-down chase camera with follow lerp, look-ahead and screen-shake. Gameplay is on the world XZ plane (Y = up, world is flat); facing is a yaw angle around +Y. Everything is data-driven and decoupled via GameState + event bus.
mechanics: Coordinate plane: gameplay on XZ plane, +Y up. pos/vel are THREE.Vector3 but y is always 0 for sim; meshes sit at y=0 (stations/asteroids may extend in y visually only). Heading `rot` is yaw in radians measured from +X toward +Z (atan2(dz,dx)). Forward unit vector = (cos rot, 0, sin rot).
Units: distance world units (wu); 1 wu ~= 1 m visually. Speed wu/s, accel wu/s^2, ang vel rad/s. Mass tonnes (t). Money credits (cr). Cargo volume u and mass t. Time seconds (s).

Fixed timestep: SIM_HZ=60, DT=1/60 s. Accumulator pattern: accumulator += min(frameTime,0.25); while(accumulator>=DT){ stepSim(DT); accumulator-=DT; }. Render alpha = accumulator/DT used to lerp pos and slerp rot between prevState and curState for smooth visuals decoupled from sim rate.

Sim step order per tick (fixed): 1 input sampling -> 2 AI/intents -> 3 flight physics integrate (thrust,drag,rotate) -> 4 weapons/projectile spawn -> 5 integrate positions (pos += vel*DT) -> 6 spatial hash rebuild -> 7 broad-phase + collision response -> 8 damage/health resolution -> 9 lifetime/TTL + alive sweep -> 10 spawn/despawn + events. Camera + render interpolation run in the render loop, NOT the sim step.

Entity storage: GameState.entities is a Map<id,Entity> plus a flat array GameState.entityList for iteration; freed ids recycled. Entities never deleted mid-step; alive=false then swept at step end (emit 'entity.destroyed').

Flight physics (semi-Newtonian): each ship has thrust force; linear drag proportional to velocity gives terminal/max speed without a hard clamp. accel = (thrustDir*thrust)/mass; vel += (accel - drag*vel)*DT. Terminal speed = thrust/(mass*drag). Drag chosen per ship so terminal == maxSpeed spec value. A hard safety clamp at 1.15*maxSpeed (2.0*maxSpeed while boosting) prevents runaway.

Rotation: ship turns toward target heading (mouse-aim world angle) at turnRate rad/s, clamped so it can't overshoot in one tick. delta = wrapAngle(targetRot - rot); step = clamp(delta, -turnRate*DT, +turnRate*DT); rot += step. angVel stored for visual/inertia bookkeeping = step/DT.

Thrust modes from input: forward (W) applies +forward thrust; back/brake (S) applies reverse thrust at 0.5x; strafe (A/D) applies side thrust at 0.6x of main. Net thrust vector summed then magnitude-clamped to thrust (boost raises cap). No thrust => only drag acts (coast & slow).

Boost/afterburner (Shift): multiplies thrust by 2.2 and raises speed clamp to 2.0x; drains boostEnergy at boostDrain/s; regenerates at boostRegen/s when not boosting and above 0. Emits 'ship.boost.start/stop' for FX/audio.

Control scheme = keyboard move + mouse-aim (chosen over twin-stick). WASD = forward/back/strafe in SHIP-LOCAL frame; mouse cursor projected onto XZ plane sets desired heading AND is the aim point weapons converge on; Shift=boost, Space=fire (handled by weapons system reading state.input). Justification below in content.

Mouse->world: raycast from camera through cursor onto the y=0 plane (THREE.Plane(0,1,0,0)) to get world aim point each frame; store in state.input.aimWorld. Desired heading = atan2(aim.z-ship.z, aim.x-ship.x).

Camera: PerspectiveCamera, fov 50. Tilt = 60 deg from horizontal (look down at 60deg; 0=flat,90=straight down). Positioned behind+above target: offset derived from zoom distance D and tilt. Default D=70 wu (height = D*sin(tilt)=60.6 wu, back = D*cos(tilt)=35 wu along -forward-ish but camera follows world up axis, not ship yaw, so the world doesn't spin under the player).

Camera follow: target = player.pos + lookAhead*velNorm*min(speed,maxSpeed)*LOOKAHEAD_K. camPosTarget = target + camOffset. camera.position.lerp(camPosTarget, 1-exp(-CAM_LERP*frameDt)) (frame-rate independent smoothing). camera.lookAt(target + shakeOffset).
Look-ahead: shifts focus point ahead of player along velocity up to LOOKAHEAD_MAX=18 wu so you see where you're going; also nudged toward mouse aim by 0.25 for situational awareness.

Screen-shake: additive trauma model. trauma in [0,1], decays trauma -= SHAKE_DECAY*dt. shakeOffset = maxShake*trauma^2*(perlin/random per axis). Hook: event bus 'camera.shake' {amount} adds trauma=min(1,trauma+amount). Used by hits, explosions, boost.

Spatial hash broad phase: uniform grid, cell=64 wu. key = floor(x/cell)+','+floor(z/cell). Rebuilt each step from entityList (skip non-colliding: pickups vs pickups, same-owner projectiles). Query 3x3 neighbor cells for each entity's candidates. O(n) for uniform density.

Collision = circle/circle on XZ using radius. Overlap if dist < rA+rB. Response depends on pair type via collision matrix: ship/ship & ship/asteroid = elastic-ish positional separation + impulse; projectile/anything(valid target) = emit 'projectile.hit' then projectile.alive=false; pickup/ship(player or trader) = emit 'pickup.collected'; ship/station = soft bounce + 'dock.range' proximity. Stations are static (mass=Infinity => immovable).

Collision impulse (equal & opposite, restitution e=0.2): n = (posB-posA)/dist; push each out by penetration*0.5 along n (scaled by inverse mass); relVel along n -> impulse j = -(1+e)*relVelN / (1/mA + 1/mB); vel adjusted by j*n/m. Static bodies use 1/m=0.

Damage pipeline (owned here as resolution, values from combat system): on 'projectile.hit'{target,damage,type} apply to shield first then hp: shield-=dmg; if shield<0 hp+=shield(neg); shield=max(0,..). if hp<=0 => alive=false, emit 'entity.killed'{id,killerId,type} (spawn system makes wreck/loot). Shields regen handled by combat/module system, not here.
Lifetime/TTL: projectiles & some FX entities carry ttl (s); ttl-=DT; ttl<=0 => alive=false. Pickups have despawnAt timestamp. Wrecks linger then fade.

Determinism: single seeded PRNG in GameState.rng (mulberry32) used by all systems for reproducibility; sim never reads Math.random directly. Sim is float-deterministic given same inputs+seed (good enough for replays/debugging, not lockstep netcode).
Pause/timescale: GameState.timeScale (default 1) multiplies how many sim steps run (set 0 to pause for menus). Render/camera keep running. Bullet-time/station-pause use this.
stateOwned: entities: Map<number,Entity> — authoritative store of all world objects
entityList: Entity[] — flat iteration array kept in sync with entities map
nextEntityId: number — monotonically increasing id allocator (recycled via freeIds)
freeIds: number[] — recycled entity id pool
playerId: number — entity id of the player ship (convenience lookup)
spatialHash: { cell:number, buckets:Map<string,number[]> } — broad-phase grid, rebuilt each step
simTime: number — total simulated seconds elapsed (authoritative clock for TTLs/despawns)
tick: number — integer sim step counter
rng: function — seeded mulberry32 PRNG; rngSeed:number stored for save/load
input: { moveX:number, moveZ:number, boost:bool, fire:bool, aimWorld:{x,z}, aimAngle:number } — sampled control state
camera: { obj:THREE.PerspectiveCamera, tilt:number, zoom:number, trauma:number, shakeOffset:Vec3, focus:Vec3, lerp:number, lookAhead:number } — camera runtime state
bounds: { radius:number, center:{x,z} } — soft sector boundary used by physics to bounce/slow entities (per-sector, set by sector system)
content: Canonical Entity shape (every object has ALL of these; system-specific data hangs off `data`): { id:number, type:string, alive:boolean, factionId:string|null, pos:THREE.Vector3(y=0), vel:THREE.Vector3(y=0), prevPos:THREE.Vector3 (for interp), rot:number(yaw rad), prevRot:number, angVel:number, radius:number(wu, collision circle), mass:number(t), hp:number, maxHp:number, shield:number, maxShield:number, thrust:number(force), turnRate:number(rad/s), maxSpeed:number(wu/s), drag:number(1/s), ttl:number|Infinity, collides:boolean, collisionMask:number(bitflags), team:number, ownerId:number|null, mesh:THREE.Object3D|null, data:object(per-type: cargo, weapons, loot, miningHardness, dockable, droneJob, etc.), flags:{boosting,docked,invuln,...} }

COLLISION MASK bitflags: SHIP=1, ASTEROID=2, STATION=4, PROJECTILE=8, PICKUP=16, DRONE=32, WRECK=64. collisionMask says what an entity collides WITH; pair collides if (A.collisionMask & B.typeBit) && (B.collisionMask & A.typeBit).

SHIP archetype stats (used by ship system; physics reads thrust/turnRate/maxSpeed/drag/mass): Scout — mass 18t, thrust 520, maxSpeed 95 wu/s, turnRate 3.4 rad/s, drag=thrust/(mass*maxSpeed)=0.304, radius 3.2, maxHp 60, maxShield 40, boostEnergy 100.
SHIP Freighter — mass 120t, thrust 1600, maxSpeed 55 wu/s, turnRate 1.3 rad/s, drag 0.242, radius 7.5, maxHp 320, maxShield 120, boostEnergy 80.
SHIP Fighter — mass 28t, thrust 1050, maxSpeed 110 wu/s, turnRate 4.2 rad/s, drag 0.341, radius 3.8, maxHp 110, maxShield 90, boostEnergy 140.
SHIP Miner — mass 60t, thrust 720, maxSpeed 60 wu/s, turnRate 1.8 rad/s, drag 0.2, radius 5.5, maxHp 180, maxShield 60, boostEnergy 90.

ASTEROID entity: type 'asteroid', mass 200-2000t (scales w/ radius), radius 6-22 wu, maxHp = radius*14, drag 0, thrust 0, turnRate 0, slow random angVel for spin (visual), data:{ore:'iron'|'copper'|'ice'|'titanium', hardness, yield}. Effectively immovable in collisions (very high mass) but can drift slowly.

STATION entity: type 'station', mass Infinity (1/m=0), radius 30-60 wu, vel 0, data:{dockRadius=radius*1.8, services:['trade','shipyard','repair'], factionId}. Emits 'dock.range' when player within dockRadius.

PROJECTILE entity: type 'projectile', radius 0.4-1.2, mass ~0 (ignored in response), ttl=range/speed, vel set at spawn = aimDir*projSpeed + 0.5*ownerVel (inherit), data:{damage,damageType,ownerId,weaponId}, collisionMask=SHIP|ASTEROID|STATION|DRONE (not other projectiles/pickups).

PICKUP entity: type 'pickup', radius 1.5, mass 1, drag 1.2 (drifts to stop), ttl/despawnAt ~60s, data:{kind:'ore'|'credits'|'cargo'|'module', amount, commodityId}, collisionMask=SHIP|DRONE. Magnet: if player within 25 wu, accelerate toward player (handled by pickup system writing vel).
DRONE entity: type 'drone', mass 6t, thrust 180, maxSpeed 70, turnRate 3.0, drag 0.43, radius 1.8, maxHp 30, data:{job:'mine'|'haul'|'escort', homeId, targetId}.
WRECK entity: type 'wreck', mass = parent*0.6, radius = parent.radius*0.8, drag 0.5, angVel random, ttl 25s (fades last 5s), data:{loot:[...]}, collisionMask=SHIP (bumpable).

CAMERA constants: fov=50, tilt=60deg, zoom/D default 70 wu (clamp 45..130 via scroll wheel -> 'camera.zoom' event), CAM_LERP=6.0 (1/s smoothing rate), LOOKAHEAD_K=0.35, LOOKAHEAD_MAX=18 wu, aimBias=0.25, near=1, far=4000.
SCREEN-SHAKE constants: SHAKE_DECAY=1.6 /s, maxShake=2.2 wu translational + 0.04 rad roll. Trauma adds: light hit 0.15, heavy hit 0.35, player explosion 0.8, boost start 0.1, asteroid mined 0.05.
SPATIAL HASH: cell=64 wu (a bit larger than biggest common collider's 2x radius for fighters/asteroids; stations handled as multi-cell insert covering their footprint).
BOUNDS: default sector radius 2500 wu; beyond it apply inward accel 40 wu/s^2 + 'leaving.sector' warning event; hard wall at 3000 wu reflects velocity.
formulas: DT = 1/60 s; clamp frameTime to <=0.25 s before accumulating
drag (per ship) so terminal==maxSpeed: drag = thrust / (mass * maxSpeed)   [units 1/s]
linear integrate: accel = thrustVec / mass ; vel += (accel - drag*vel) * DT ; pos += vel * DT
terminal/max speed (no boost): vTerm = thrust / (mass * drag) == maxSpeed
boost: effThrust = thrust * 2.2 ; speedClamp = (boosting ? 2.0 : 1.15) * maxSpeed ; if |vel|>speedClamp: vel.setLength(speedClamp)
rotation toward target: d = atan2(sin(tRot-rot),cos(tRot-rot)) ; rot += clamp(d, -turnRate*DT, turnRate*DT) ; angVel = clampedStep/DT
wrapAngle(a) = atan2(sin a, cos a)  (keeps headings in [-pi,pi])
forward unit = (cos rot, 0, sin rot) ; right unit = (cos(rot+pi/2),0,sin(rot+pi/2)) = (-sin rot,0,cos rot)
local thrust compose: T = forward*(W?1:0 - S?0.5:0)*thrust + right*((D?1:0)-(A?1:0))*0.6*thrust ; if |T|>effThrust: T.setLength(effThrust)
mouse->world: ray = raycaster(camera, ndcMouse); t = -ray.origin.y / ray.dir.y ; aimWorld = ray.origin + ray.dir*t  (intersect plane y=0)
desired heading: tRot = atan2(aimWorld.z - ship.pos.z, aimWorld.x - ship.pos.x)
camera offset from tilt: camOffset = (0, D*sin(tilt), -D*cos(tilt)) in WORLD axes (fixed, world-up; camera does not yaw with ship)
frame-rate-independent lerp factor: a = 1 - exp(-CAM_LERP * frameDt) ; camera.position.lerp(targetPos, a)
look-ahead focus: focus = player.pos + velDir*min(speed,maxSpeed)*LOOKAHEAD_K (capped at LOOKAHEAD_MAX) + (aimWorld - player.pos)*aimBias
screen shake: trauma = max(0, trauma - SHAKE_DECAY*frameDt) ; s = trauma^2 ; shake = (rand[-1,1]*maxShake*s, 0, rand[-1,1]*maxShake*s) ; cameraRoll = rand[-1,1]*0.04*s
render interpolation: renderPos = lerp(prevPos, pos, alpha) ; renderRot = rot - wrapAngle(rot-prevRot)*(1-alpha) ; alpha = accumulator/DT
spatial hash key: cx=floor(x/cell), cz=floor(z/cell), key = cx*73856093 ^ cz*19349663 (or string `${cx},${cz}`)
collision overlap: dx=bx-ax, dz=bz-az, d2=dx*dx+dz*dz, R=ra+rb ; collide if d2 < R*R ; d=sqrt(d2)
penetration separation: pen = R - d ; n=(dx,dz)/d ; invSum = invMassA+invMassB ; A.pos -= n*pen*(invMassA/invSum) ; B.pos += n*pen*(invMassB/invSum)
impulse: relV = (B.vel-A.vel)·n ; if relV<0: j = -(1+e)*relV / invSum ; A.vel -= n*(j*invMassA) ; B.vel += n*(j*invMassB)  with e=0.2
invMass = (mass===Infinity ? 0 : 1/mass)
damage apply: absorbed = min(shield, dmg) ; shield -= absorbed ; hp -= (dmg - absorbed) ; if hp<=0 alive=false
projectile spawn vel: vel = aimDir*projSpeed + ownerVel*0.5 ; ttl = weaponRange/projSpeed
boost energy: if boosting: boostEnergy -= boostDrain*DT (stop at 0) else boostEnergy = min(max, boostEnergy + boostRegen*DT)  [boostDrain 28/s, boostRegen 16/s]
sector bounds: r = dist(pos,center) ; if r>boundRadius: vel += (center-pos)/r * 40 * DT ; if r>hardRadius: reflect vel about radial normal
interactions: EMITS 'entity.destroyed' {id,type} — render removes mesh, pools freed (swept at step end)
EMITS 'entity.killed' {id, killerId, type, pos, faction} — spawn system makes wreck/loot, combat awards bounty/xp, mission system checks objectives
EMITS 'projectile.hit' {targetId, ownerId, damage, damageType, pos} — combat resolves damage (or this system applies it directly), FX/audio play impact
EMITS 'collision' {aId, bId, impulse, pos} — audio plays clunk, FX sparks, damage-on-ram handled by combat
EMITS 'pickup.collected' {pickupId, collectorId, kind, amount, commodityId} — cargo/economy system credits the player/trader
EMITS 'dock.range' {stationId, shipId, inRange:bool} — station/UI system shows dock prompt
EMITS 'camera.shake.applied' (internal) and LISTENS 'camera.shake' {amount} — any system requests shake (weapons, explosions, mining, boost)
EMITS 'ship.boost.start' / 'ship.boost.stop' {shipId} — audio engine pitch, FX thruster flare
EMITS 'leaving.sector' {shipId, dist} — UI warning, AI turn-back
LISTENS 'camera.zoom' {delta} — mouse wheel adjusts camera.zoom within clamp

LISTENS 'entity.spawn.request' {spec} — central factory creates an Entity from data spec, assigns id, inserts into entities/entityList, emits 'entity.spawned' (this is the canonical creation path all systems call)
LISTENS 'entity.kill' {id, killerId} — force-kill an entity (mission scripts, console)
READS state.input (written by input system each frame) for player ship intent; ai system writes equivalent intent fields onto NPC entities' data.intent which physics reads
PROVIDES helpers consumed everywhere: spawnEntity(spec), getEntity(id), queryRadius(pos,r)->Entity[] (uses spatial hash), forEachInCells, raycastToPlane(ndc), worldToScreen(pos) (for DOM HUD markers)
WRITES to save/load: serializeState() dumps entities (minus mesh refs), simTime, tick, rngSeed, camera zoom; loadState() rebuilds entities and re-emits 'entity.spawned' so render re-creates meshes
Update order dependency: input -> ai -> THIS(physics/integrate/collision) -> weapons read positions -> economy/cargo on pickup events -> render/camera(interp) last
uiNeeds: worldToScreen(entity.pos) projection helper so the DOM HUD overlay can place off-screen target arrows, enemy/loot markers, station icons, and damage numbers anchored to world entities each frame
Player vitals feed: hp/maxHp, shield/maxShield, boostEnergy/max, current speed (|vel|) and maxSpeed for HUD bars/throttle readout
Boost/afterburner state + cooldown/energy for a HUD energy bar and thruster glow
Camera zoom level indicator (optional) and a 'recenter' affordance
Dock prompt trigger: when 'dock.range' inRange true, HUD shows 'Press F to dock' near the station marker
Sector-bounds warning banner driven by 'leaving.sector' (distance + direction-to-center arrow)
Screen-shake must NOT move DOM HUD (HUD is screen-space, immune); only the 3D camera shakes — UI layer documents this so HUD stays readable
Minimap/radar feed: queryRadius around player returns nearby entities with type/faction for the DOM radar widget
Pause overlay hook: when timeScale=0 show menu; sim frozen but camera/UI live
risks: Plane-axis consistency (XZ, +Y up, yaw from +X toward +Z): every other subsystem must agree or aim/movement/camera desync. Document the forward-vector convention once and reuse the same helper.
Camera must follow position but NOT ship yaw (world-locked orientation) or the starfield/world spins under the player and induces nausea; keep look-ahead subtle.

Render interpolation requires prevPos/prevRot snapshot at the START of each sim step; forgetting to update them, or interpolating across teleports/spawns, causes visual streaking — flag 'noInterp' on spawn/teleport frames.
Drag-based max speed means changing thrust without recomputing drag silently changes top speed; always derive drag from thrust/mass/maxSpeed at ship build time (or recompute on module change).

Spatial hash cell size vs largest collider: stations (radius up to 60) exceed one 64-wu cell — must insert static large bodies into all overlapped cells or they'll miss collisions; alternatively keep stations in a separate static list queried specially.

Projectiles are fast/small — at 60Hz a fast bullet can tunnel through thin/small targets between steps; mitigate with swept circle test (segment prevPos->pos vs target circle) for projectiles, not just point overlap.
Equal-mass elastic response with asteroids set to finite huge mass can jitter; treat asteroids/stations as invMass~0 to avoid the player shoving a mountain.
Frame-rate-independent lerp (1-exp(-k*dt)) is correct; naive lerp(a,b,k*dt) is not and will feel different at different FPS — use the exp form.
timeScale>1 (fast-forward) runs many sim steps per frame; cap steps per frame (e.g. <=8) to avoid spiral-of-death when tab regains focus, and clamp frameTime to 0.25s.
Determinism: any system reading Math.random instead of state.rng breaks replay/save reproducibility — enforce via a single rng and code review.
Entity 'data' bag is a coupling risk: keep per-type payloads namespaced and documented so systems don't collide on field names.
Mesh lifetime: never delete a THREE mesh inside the sim step (render owns GL resources); only set alive=false and let render dispose on 'entity.destroyed'.
